# UC-23.1.3 · Real-Time Page Load Performance Impact on Revenue

## Description

Joins per-session Core Web Vitals from a bespoke `web:rum` HEC feed (Largest Contentful Paint, Interaction to Next Paint, Cumulative Layout Shift, Time to First Byte) with completed purchases on `session_id`, buckets LCP/INP/CLS at Google’s March 2024 field thresholds, then aggregates conversion rate and revenue per session by `page_template` and LCP-backed performance slice. Surfaces cohort gaps versus template-wide conversion averages and a headline revenue-uplift estimate `(sessions × (overall_cvr − cvr) / 100 × AOV)` style math using observed revenue-per-conversion—useful for CDN budgets, quarterly performance-engineering OKRs, and before/after optimisation attribution while acknowledging traffic-mix confounders.

## Value

Benchmark literature ties latency to commerce outcomes—Walmart Labs (~2012) roughly ~2% conversion lift per second improved; Akamai (~2017) retail experiments near ~7% conversion drop per ~100 ms delay; Amazon (~2008) often cited ~1% sales sensitivity per ~100 ms; Pinterest (~2017) tied shorter waits to materially higher sign-ups; Google (~2017) highlighted majority mobile abandonment beyond ~3 s loads; Cloudflare (2024) reported median p75 LCP near ~2.85 s with large CWV failure rates. This use case monetises those narratives on your ledger: Poor LCP cohorts carry quantified uplift potential beside p75 paint and pass-rate counters, giving engineering a revenue-backed brief for edge, image pipeline, and tag-management work—distinct from funnel-stage counts (UC-23.1.1) or cart-abandonment recovery (UC-23.1.2).

## Implementation

Instrument `web-vitals` (`onLCP`, `onINP`, `onCLS`, `onTTFB`) into JSON POST or `navigator.sendBeacon` toward HEC (`index=business`, `sourcetype=web:rum`) with stable `session_id`, `page_template`, millisecond-normalised metrics, and bot-aware filtering upstream or in SPL; ingest Apache `%D` microseconds and NGINX `$request_time` via Splunkbase 3186/4767 for origin corroboration; emit `web:order_complete` with matching `session_id` and `order_value`. Schedule weekly steering extracts and clone panels by optional `geo_continent`, `device_type`, or `experiment_variant_id` once fields exist.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud Search Heads need roles granting `index=business` (retarget every `index=` token together). Heavy Forwarder or Cloud HEC endpoints accept HTTPS POST bodies from your storefront collector path (`/api/rum-collector` forwarded into Splunk) with TLS profiles carriers accept on Safari mobile. Parsing tiers host Splunk Add-on for Apache Web Server (Splunkbase 3186) and Splunk Add-on for NGINX (Splunkbase 4767) so `access_combined` and nginx fragments inherit TIMESTAMP extraction without duplicate LINE_BREAKER churn. Budget ingest for uncompressed JSON bursts during promotions—beacon storms exceed steady-state flat-file tails. Coordinate `session_id` naming with whichever system emits `web:order_complete` so the left join retains purchase rows across SPA transitions and payment redirects.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Browser path: bundle the `web-vitals` library and emit one event per callback with fields `metric`, `value`, `rating`, `page_path`, `page_template`, `session_id`, optional `cart_value`, `connection_type`, and millisecond-compatible timestamps; ship JSON through Splunk HTTP Event Collector using `/services/collector/event` into `index=business`, `sourcetype=web:rum`. Where policy blocks direct browser-to-Splunk posts, terminate at an API gateway that authenticates bearer tokens, forwards to HEC, and stamps `_time` from collector receipt unless you have proven NTP alignment for client `timestamp` fields. Optional enterprise path: initialise Splunk Observability RUM (`splunkRum.init`) targeting OTLP ingest `https://rum-ingest.<realm>.signalfx.com/v1/rumotlp`, then land condensed aggregates back in Enterprise via Splunk APM Add-on—keep Platform ownership because dashboards prioritise commerce uplift narratives.

Apache origin logs must include `%D` microsecond durations in `CustomLog` formats—divide by one thousand at search-time for millisecond overlays; avoid `%T`, which truncates to integer seconds and collapses fast routes to fake zeros. NGINX deployments should retain `$request_time` (fractional seconds) per Splunkbase 4767 guidance and, where blame-splitting matters, capture `$upstream_response_time` alongside so slow mobile uploads reading POST bodies do not masquerade as PHP stalls.

Commerce outcomes publish `web:order_complete` rows keyed by the same `session_id` strings emitted inside beacon payloads—whether Shopify webhook enrichment, Stripe Checkout callbacks bridged through Kafka to HEC, or ERP acknowledgement loops—and strip PAN-like payloads at ingestion.

Step 2 — Create the saved search
Reuse the SPL shown in the `spl` field verbatim for initial rollout—adjust only token names after validating `_raw` in Interactive Search. The opening stanza collapses repeated beacon deliveries per `session_id` by taking `max()` per metric within the thirty-day window so duplicate retries widen totals rather than multiply sessions. `lcp_bucket`, `inp_bucket`, and `cls_bucket` encode Google March 2024 field thresholds; `cwv_pass` marks sessions passing all three Good buckets simultaneously for headline pass-rate tiles. The subsearch aggregates purchases once per session (`sum(order_value)`, `max(1) as purchased`) before the left join; widen purchase latency separately if ERP batches arrive outside tight windows—see troubleshooting on attribution lag. Downstream `stats` aggregates sessions and dollars into `(lcp_bucket, page_template)` cells; `eventstats` computes template-wide average conversion so `cvr_delta_vs_avg` exposes under-performing Poor slices; `revenue_uplift_potential` applies uplift mathematics equivalent to average-order-value scaled by sessions and conversion gap—guard `conversions>0` to dodge divide-by-null noise.

Step 3 — Validate field realism
Verify in the PageSpeed Insights UI that field-tab Largest Contentful Paint bands for canonical URLs align directionally with Splunk `lcp_bucket` trends after time-window alignment; cross-check Chrome UX Report dashboard medians when URLs qualify for CrUX coverage. Spot-check ten Splunk sessions flagged Poor against DevTools Performance traces on matched templates when reproducible—ensure beacon timing aligns with Largest Contentful Paint selection rules your SPA implements.

Compare Splunk dollar totals weekly against Shopify Analytics, Adobe Commerce dashboards, or ERP settlement extracts inside tolerance bands documented beside fiscal calendars—timezone normalization near UTC midnight splits totals slightly unless `_time` aligns with commerce closes.

Step 4 — Operationalise reporting cadence
Publish Dashboard Studio layouts pairing scatter plots (p75 Largest Contentful Paint versus cohort conversion rate), heatmaps coloured by revenue, uplift trendlines with annotated promo shutters, and single-value widgets for fleet p75 Largest Contentful Paint week-over-week movement plus Core Web Vitals pass-rate share. Governance: Performance Engineering owns quarterly OKRs sourced from uplift totals; commerce analytics consumes monthly steering slices segmented once optional dimensions (`geo_continent`, `device_type`, `experiment_variant_id`) exist.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
No `web:rum` events during rollout — validate `srchIndexesAllowed` lists `business`, confirm HEC tokens lock to that index, and tail Heavy Forwarder `splunkd.log` for HTTP Event Collector lines showing token rejection or SSL handshake failures when Safari carriers pin unexpected cipher suites.

Apache `%D` versus `%T`: `%D` reports microseconds (example 245132); `%T` reports whole seconds—teams relying on `%T` falsely bucket micro-fast routes as zero-second latency until `%D` restores fidelity.

NGINX `$request_time` versus upstream timers: `$request_time` spans client read phases—slow mobile uploads inflate totals one hundred to five hundred milliseconds beyond backend compute; compare `$upstream_response_time` when isolating PHP, Java, or origin API blame.

CDN edge cache divergence: Cloudflare or Akamai HIT responses often paint in tens of milliseconds while origin logs only see MISS traffic at hundreds of milliseconds—blending solely from `access_combined` understates shopper-visible wins whenever cache ratios spike.

`navigator.sendBeacon` during unload on Safari iOS releases prior to fifteen reportedly drops thirty to forty percent of terminal posts—flush Interaction to Next Paint and Cumulative Layout Shift on `pagehide` rather than `beforeunload`, or Poor cohort narratives skew versus Chrome.

Bot and synthetic contamination: Googlebot, Lighthouse CI, GTmetrix, Catchpoint, Datadog Synthetics, and New Relic Synthetics paints inflate Good tails unless user-agent filters exclude `(?i)bot|spider|lighthouse|gtmetrix|webpagetest|pingdom|catchpoint|datadogsynthetics|newrelicsyn`.

Single-page-application Interaction to Next Paint: INP spans the entire tab lifetime—route-to-route navigations never reset the metric, so late-session spikes may reflect unrelated flows unless measurement scopes tighten.

Chrome prerender and back-forward cache: speculative renders may yield zero-millisecond Largest Contentful Paint unless Performance Navigation Timing discard markers filter fictitious Good saturation.

Apple Mail Privacy Protection prefetch may spawn phantom beacon sessions lacking purchases—carry `via_email_prefetch` flags from mail headers before interpreting abandonment deltas.

Geographic latency confounding: Asia-Pacific shoppers routinely carry higher p75 Largest Contentful Paint solely from propagation distance—aggregate continents separately (`geo_continent`) before commissioning POP spend.

Connection-type bias: `navigator.connection.effectiveType="2g"` shoppers legitimately skew Poor—aggregate separately from Wi-Fi cohorts when diagnosing optimisations.

Mobile versus desktop splits: handset p75 Largest Contentful Paint is often one-point-five to two times desktop—blended dashboards hide handset failure while desktops look healthy.

CDN failover incidents: historical operator incidents (example June twenty twenty-one routing shifts) mimic onsite regressions until CDN status timelines corroborate Splunk overlays.

Marketing pixel cascades through tag managers inflate Largest Contentful Paint via sequential third-party downloads—trace initiator chains in browser devtools and prioritise `defer`, `async`, or Cloudflare Rocket Loader adjustments.

Conversion attribution lag: purchases firing beyond conservative thirty-minute joins drop out—experiment with `bin _time span=1h` around joins or widen commerce receipts before declaring uplift unattributable.

Experiment skew: Optimizely, Adobe Target, or LaunchDarkly variants intentionally shift paint budgets—segment `experiment_variant_id` before citing regression.

Browser-milestone regressions: Chrome releases occasionally shift Interaction to Next Paint unrelated to storefront bundles—segment `chrome_major_version` overlays alongside Splunk curves.

Black Friday-style overload: Elastic Load Balancer surge queues and exhausted Apache worker pools lift Time to First Byte into seconds without code churn—overlay ELB `SurgeQueueLength`, HAProxy backlog timers, or BusyWorkers dashboards whenever uplift spikes coincide with sustained checkout bursts.

## SPL

```spl
index=business sourcetype="web:rum" metric IN ("LCP","INP","CLS","TTFB") earliest=-30d
| stats max(eval(if(metric="LCP",value,null()))) as lcp_ms,
        max(eval(if(metric="INP",value,null()))) as inp_ms,
        max(eval(if(metric="CLS",value,null()))) as cls_score,
        max(eval(if(metric="TTFB",value,null()))) as ttfb_ms,
        values(page_template) as page_template,
        values(connection_type) as connection_type
   by session_id
| eval lcp_bucket=case(
    lcp_ms<=2500, "Good",
    lcp_ms<=4000, "Needs Improvement",
    1=1, "Poor"),
    inp_bucket=case(
    inp_ms<=200, "Good",
    inp_ms<=500, "Needs Improvement",
    1=1, "Poor"),
    cls_bucket=case(
    cls_score<=0.1, "Good",
    cls_score<=0.25, "Needs Improvement",
    1=1, "Poor")
| eval cwv_pass=if(lcp_bucket="Good" AND inp_bucket="Good" AND cls_bucket="Good", 1, 0)
| join type=left session_id [
    search index=business sourcetype="web:order_complete" earliest=-30d
    | stats sum(order_value) as revenue, max(1) as purchased by session_id]
| fillnull value=0 revenue purchased
| stats count as sessions, sum(purchased) as conversions, sum(revenue) as revenue,
        avg(lcp_ms) as avg_lcp_ms, perc75(lcp_ms) as p75_lcp_ms,
        avg(inp_ms) as avg_inp_ms, perc75(inp_ms) as p75_inp_ms
   by lcp_bucket, page_template
| eval cvr=if(sessions>0, round(100*conversions/sessions, 2), null())
| eval rev_per_session=if(sessions>0, round(revenue/sessions, 2), null())
| eventstats sum(sessions) as total_sessions, avg(cvr) as overall_cvr by page_template
| eval cvr_delta_vs_avg=round(cvr-overall_cvr, 2)
| eval revenue_uplift_potential=if(lcp_bucket="Poor" AND overall_cvr>cvr AND conversions>0,
    round(sessions*((overall_cvr-cvr)/100)*(revenue/conversions), 0), 0)
| sort page_template, lcp_bucket
| table page_template, lcp_bucket, sessions, p75_lcp_ms, conversions, cvr, cvr_delta_vs_avg, rev_per_session, revenue_uplift_potential
```

## Visualization

Scatter plot (horizontal: p75 LCP ms by `page_template`, vertical: cohort conversion rate `%`, bubble size ∝ sessions); bar chart (conversion rate `%` split by `lcp_bucket`, trellis or colour by `page_template`); heatmap (`page_template` × `lcp_bucket` cells shaded by revenue sum); single-value row (fleet p75 LCP with week-over-week delta, companion tile for `%` sessions where `cwv_pass=1`, optional tertiary badge colour); trend chart (seven-day rolling sum of `revenue_uplift_potential` by `page_template`, annotate major promos).
